text:
Most AI products should not start with automation.

They should start with deletion.

What step can disappear completely?

Not faster.
Not prettier.
Gone.

Example:
If a small business still has to copy supplier data into Excel after using your tool, you did not automate the
name: ⚡️Korba.tgn⚡️

text: AI use case in 2026: AI agents managing small business operations in 2026. Practical automation is moving from demo to daily workflow. #AI #Automation #Technology #FutureOfWork #2026 https://t.co/v80dH5M6Dg
name: Daniel Reitberg ・Official ・Joined 2008

text:
Most AI automation fails because people start with tools.

Start with one leaking workflow:
- trigger
- inputs
- AI draft
- human approval
- output
- review loop

Then automate the smallest useful version.

What workflow should a small business fix first?
name: Herniel K

text: Stop basic prompts. 🛑 Most miss real AI automation for small business: Standard, Agents, & MCP. Sneaky sneaky workflow automation. 😉 Sorry competitors! 📉 Grab the edge: https://t.co/aOghwRLoHi #AIagents https://t.co/d9y59UvcBH
name: The Good Fellas Agency

text:
🤖 A useful AI workflow should make verification easier, not harder.

For a small business, that means clear handoffs:
• what AI drafted
• what data it used
• what a human approves

Trust is part of the automation.
name: Happy Monkey AI

text:
I’ve got room this week for one quick AI/workflow build for a small business.

Examples:
• intake form → spreadsheet/CRM
• invoice or field-ticket automation
• simple customer portal
• AI assistant for FAQs or lead capture
• internal dashboard / lightweight app

Fast
name: Max 

text: AI automation gets real when a workflow stops being a chat and becomes an inspectable loop. For a small business, the value is not one more prompt. It is checkpoints, memory, validation, and clear handoff points so the agent can keep moving without creating hidden risk.
name: Sunrise Lee
